message:
feat(native_events): Cx-first NativeEventListener::bind_with_cx (ft-xbnl0.2.3)

Close native_events.rs's last cx gap on NativeEventListener bind.

`bind_with_cx(cx, socket_path)` threads the caller's cx into the
setup sequence with 4 checkpoint seams before each filesystem /
syscall boundary:
- At entry (before empty-path validation)
- Before stale-socket cleanup
- Before parent-dir creation
- Before listener bind

Gives responsive cancellation during socket setup — a cx-driven
startup path cancelled mid-bind won't touch files it doesn't need to.
Cancellation surfaces as `NativeEventError::Io(Interrupted)` so
existing caller error-match arms continue to hold.

Legacy `bind` preserved unchanged.

Two regression tests pin the contract:
- `bind_with_cx_succeeds_on_fresh_cx` — fresh cx produces a live
  listener and materialized socket file.
- `bind_with_precancelled_cx_returns_interrupted` — pre-cancelled
  cx returns `NativeEventError::Io(Interrupted)` with "cancelled"
  in the message, and the socket file is NOT created.

Verified via fork bypass (CC=llvm clang, CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/
ft-rusticmaple-target, --features native-wezterm,asupersync-runtime):
78/78 native_events tests pass.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
